## Summary

Completes the WAVE readiness doctrine hardening and adds mandatory PR trust-proof enforcement.

**Core doctrine: WAVE can diagnose and plan. Revise repairs. Quality Gate validates.**

This PR does not claim the evaluation process is fixed merely because labels are fixed. It closes the remaining active runtime/UI surfaces that still said "WAVE Revision," and adds a PR-level validator so every future UI, UX, repo, code, docs, infra, and migration PR proves the required trust conditions before it is considered done.

Process Change: no — naming/governance only; no scoring, no model calls, no pipeline behavior change.

---

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Fix remaining active log strings in `lib/evaluation/waveRevision.ts` ("starting revision phase", "Revision phase complete") that still implied WAVE = repair workflow
- Add `__tests__/governance/waveReadinessNamingContract.test.ts` — scans app/components/lib for forbidden active-surface WAVE doctrine violations
- Add `scripts/validate-pr-trust-proof.mjs` — requires PR bodies to prove all trust conditions
- Add `.github/workflows/pr-trust-proof.yml` — enforces trust-proof validator on every PR (open, edit, sync, reopen, ready_for_review)
- Add typed PR templates: ui, code, docs, infra, migration — each includes all required trust-proof sections

**Out of scope:**
- Scoring logic
- Model prompt changes
- Provider calls
- DB migrations
- Artifact type renames (`wave_revision_plan_v1`, `wave_revision`)
- WAVE execution behavior
- Revise Queue implementation
- TrustedPath implementation
- Quality Gate behavior
- Internal identifier renames (`executeWaveRevision`, `WaveRevisionPlanArtifact`, `WaveRevisionResult`, `WaveRevisionProofDecision`)

---

## Correct Doctrine

```
WAVE is not Pass 4.
WAVE is not the Revise Queue.
WAVE is not TrustedPath.
WAVE is the final long-form evaluation/readiness layer before the report and revision handoff.
WAVE may produce revision intelligence.
Revise performs the repair workflow.
```

Pipeline sequence:
```
Submit manuscript
  ↓ Phase 0 — Governance Warmup
  ↓ Chunk Routing Manifest
  ↓ Track B (Phase 1A Story Layer) + Track C (Pass 3A Preflight Scout) [concurrent]
  ↓ Review Gate
  ↓ Phase 2 — Criteria Analysis
  ↓ Phase 3B — Synthesis
  ↓ Quality Gate        ← deterministic validation; NOT WAVE
  ↓ WAVE Readiness Layer ← long-form readiness / revision-planning analysis; NOT Pass 4; NOT repair
  ↓ DREAM / Longform Report
  ↓ Complete
  ↓ Revise Queue / TrustedPath  ← repair workflow; begins only after eval/report artifacts exist
```

---

## Files Changed

### Runtime fix
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `lib/evaluation/waveRevision.ts` | Log strings: "starting revision phase" → "starting WAVE readiness layer"; "Revision phase complete" → "WAVE readiness layer complete"; doc comment for `resolveWaveSourceVersionId` updated |

### Governance additions
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `__tests__/governance/waveReadinessNamingContract.test.ts` | Scans app/components/lib for forbidden active-surface WAVE doctrine violations; fails CI if any are found |
| `scripts/validate-pr-trust-proof.mjs` | Validates PR bodies contain all 8 required trust-proof sections with non-trivial content |
| `.github/workflows/pr-trust-proof.yml` | Runs trust-proof validator + naming contract on every PR; applies to mixed-scope PRs |
| `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/ui.md` | Typed PR template with trust-proof sections |
| `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/code.md` | Typed PR template with trust-proof sections |
| `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/docs.md` | Typed PR template with trust-proof sections |
| `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/infra.md` | Typed PR template with trust-proof sections |
| `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/migration.md` | Typed PR template with trust-proof sections |

### Prior commits (already on main, not re-landed here)
Round 1 (5 files): `progressLabels.ts`, `waveRevisionProof.ts`, `waveRevision.ts` (doc), `WAVE_GUIDE.ts`, `route.ts`
Round 2 (3 files): `phase-helpers.ts`, `waveProgressPatch.ts`, `processor.ts` (markRunning + message strings)

---

## Input → Action → Output

**Input:**
- Remaining active log strings in `waveRevision.ts` still saying "starting revision phase" / "Revision phase complete" — operator-visible, implies WAVE = repair phase
- No PR governance mechanism enforcing the trust-proof contract on UI/UX/repo/code/docs/infra/migration PRs
- No typed PR templates with required trust-proof sections
- No naming contract test to prevent doctrine regressions from re-entering active surfaces

**Action:**
- Normalize the final two active log strings to "starting WAVE readiness layer" / "WAVE readiness layer complete"
- Add a naming contract test that will fail CI if forbidden strings return to app/components/lib
- Add a trust-proof validator that requires PR bodies to prove all 8 conditions
- Add typed templates and workflow enforcement for all PR types including mixed-scope

**Output:**
- All active runtime/UI surfaces correctly distinguish: Quality Gate (deterministic validation) → WAVE Readiness Layer (diagnoses/plans) → Revise Queue/TrustedPath (repairs)
- Future PRs cannot be considered done without proving trust conditions
- Internal compatibility identifiers remain stable; no migration required

---

## Unauthorized Input Sources

No unauthorized input sources are introduced.

This PR adds no provider calls, external fetches, model inputs, new manuscript sources, third-party APIs, environment variable reads at runtime, or new data ingestion paths. The trust-proof validator reads only the PR body (a static string injected by GitHub Actions). The naming contract test reads local source files at test time, not at runtime.

---

## Internal Process Leakage

No internal process leakage is introduced.

The wording fixes replace log strings that operators see in Vercel/Supabase logs — these now say "WAVE readiness layer" instead of "revision phase," which is safer language that does not imply the manuscript is being repaired by the evaluation engine. No private chain-of-thought, model reasoning, governance traces, raw artifact content, or internal scoring diagnostics are exposed.

---

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

Public-safe status language used in active surfaces after this PR:

| Surface | Label |
|---|---|
| Progress message (markRunning) | "Running WAVE readiness analysis" |
| Terminal message | "WAVE readiness layer complete" |
| Phase helper (UI) | "Preparing WAVE readiness plan…" |
| Log (operator-facing) | "[WAVE] starting WAVE readiness layer" |
| Log (operator-facing) | "[WAVE] WAVE readiness layer complete" |

None of these expose internal model names, prompt versions, artifact schema fields, or governance chain-of-thought.

---

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

No hidden runtime or pipeline expansion.

This PR does not add evaluation phases, model calls, provider calls, DB migrations, artifact types, scoring logic, WAVE modules, or revision execution paths. The only behavioral change is log string wording. The naming contract test and trust-proof validator run in CI only.

---

## Latency Impact

No unnecessary latency increase.

Expected latency impact: zero at runtime. CI adds two lightweight jobs (trust-proof validator: ~2s, naming contract test: ~10s). No evaluation, worker, or API latency is affected.

---

## Post-Merge Sanity Sweep

After merging to main:

```bash
git checkout main && git pull --ff-only

# 1. Verify no forbidden active-surface strings remain
grep -rn \
  "WAVE Revision\|WAVE revision complete\|Preparing WAVE revision plan\|starting revision phase\|Revision phase complete\|repair through WAVE\|WAVE repairs\|WAVE is Pass 4\|Pass 4 status" \
  app components lib \
  --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" \
  --exclude-dir=node_modules \
  --exclude-dir=__tests__ \
  --exclude-dir=tests

# 2. Run naming contract test
npx jest __tests__/governance/waveReadinessNamingContract.test.ts --runInBand --forceExit --no-coverage

# 3. Run trust-proof validator against this PR body
cat .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE/code.md | node scripts/validate-pr-trust-proof.mjs

# 4. Verify workflow YAML syntax (actionlint if available)
# actionlint .github/workflows/pr-trust-proof.yml
```

Expected:
- grep returns zero hits
- Naming contract: all tests pass
- Trust-proof validator: PASSED
- Actionlint: unproven locally until CI runs

---

## Actionlint Status

Full actionlint validation remains unproven in this environment until CI proves it.

The `.github/workflows/pr-trust-proof.yml` workflow has been hand-reviewed for correct syntax: `on:` trigger, `jobs:` structure, `steps:` ordering, `uses:` pinned to `@v4`, and the `node` invocation. CI will validate workflow syntax when this PR runs its first check. If actionlint fails in CI, the fix is syntax-only and does not require a logic change.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Active runtime/UI surfaces no longer say "WAVE Revision," "WAVE revision complete," or "Preparing WAVE revision plan"
- [x] `waveRevision.ts` log strings say "starting WAVE readiness layer" and "WAVE readiness layer complete"
- [x] WAVE is described as the long-form readiness / revision-planning analysis layer
- [x] Revise Queue and TrustedPath remain the only repair workflows
- [x] Quality Gate remains deterministic validation; not renamed to WAVE or Pass 4
- [x] Internal compatibility identifiers (`wave_revision`, `wave_revision_plan_v1`, `executeWaveRevision`) are not renamed in this PR
- [x] `__tests__/governance/waveReadinessNamingContract.test.ts` exists and fails on forbidden active-surface wording
- [x] `scripts/validate-pr-trust-proof.mjs` exists and requires all 8 trust-proof sections
- [x] Typed PR templates exist for UI, code, docs, infra, and migration
- [x] `.github/workflows/pr-trust-proof.yml` invokes trust-proof validator and naming contract on all PRs
- [x] Mixed-scope PRs pass through the same trust-proof validator (single workflow, no template-type routing escape)
- [x] PR body proves: no unauthorized input sources
- [x] PR body proves: no internal process leakage
- [x] PR body proves: clear Input → Action → Output
- [x] PR body proves: public-safe quality/status metrics
- [x] PR body proves: no hidden runtime/pipeline expansion
- [x] PR body proves: no unnecessary latency increase
- [x] Post-merge sanity sweep instructions are included
- [x] Full actionlint validation explicitly marked unproven until CI proves it
